notes.content:
**Magma: A Foundation Model for Multimodal AI Agents** (CVPR 2025; arxiv:2502.13130)
- Contribution: Single foundation model for both digital + physical action.
- Methods: Set-of-Mark (SoM) for action grounding; Trace-of-Mark (ToM) for planning.
- Results: SOTA on UI navigation + robot manipulation benchmarks.
- Limitations: Zero-shot real-robot dexterous performance lags specialist VLAs.

meta.title: Magma: A Foundation Model for Multimodal AI Agents
meta.key_contribution: Single foundation model for both digital + physical action.
meta.methods: Set-of-Mark (SoM) for action grounding; Trace-of-Mark (ToM) for planning.
meta.results: SOTA on UI navigation + robot manipulation benchmarks.
meta.limitations: Zero-shot real-robot dexterous performance lags specialist VLAs.

notes.content:
**OpenVLA: An Open-Source Vision-Language-Action Model** (arXiv 2024; arxiv:2406.09246)
- Contribution: First open-weights VLA matching or beating proprietary RT-2 baselines.
- Methods: Fine-tunes Llama 2 + SigLIP/DINOv2 visual encoder on tokenized 7-DoF action sequences.
- Results: +20.4% over Diffusion Policy across multi-task multi-object generalization; LoRA-tuneable on consumer GPUs.
- Limitations: 5 Hz inference is too slow for dexterous control; discrete action tokens limit precision; single-image observation hurts depth tasks.

meta.title: OpenVLA: An Open-Source Vision-Language-Action Model
meta.key_contribution: First open-weights VLA matching or beating proprietary RT-2 baselines.
meta.methods: Fine-tunes Llama 2 + SigLIP/DINOv2 visual encoder on tokenized 7-DoF action sequences.
meta.results: +20.4% over Diffusion Policy across multi-task multi-object generalization; LoRA-tuneable on consumer GPUs.
meta.limitations: 5 Hz inference is too slow for dexterous control; discrete action tokens limit precision; single-image observation hurts depth tasks.

notes.content:
**GR00T N1: An Open Foundation Model for Generalist Humanoid Robots** (arXiv 2025; arxiv:2503.14734)
- Contribution: First open humanoid foundation model with embedded-GPU latency budget.
- Methods: Dual-system: VLM at 5-10 Hz + visuomotor diffusion-transformer at 100+ Hz.
- Results: GR00T-N1-2B samples 16 actions in 63.9 ms on an L40 GPU.
- Limitations: Narrow real-world dexterity benchmarks; sim2real still needs domain-randomization tuning.

meta.title: GR00T N1: An Open Foundation Model for Generalist Humanoid Robots
meta.key_contribution: First open humanoid foundation model with embedded-GPU latency budget.
meta.methods: Dual-system: VLM at 5-10 Hz + visuomotor diffusion-transformer at 100+ Hz.
meta.results: GR00T-N1-2B samples 16 actions in 63.9 ms on an L40 GPU.
meta.limitations: Narrow real-world dexterity benchmarks; sim2real still needs domain-randomization tuning.

notes.content:
**Gemini Robotics: Bringing AI into the Physical World** (arXiv 2025; arxiv:2503.20020)
- Contribution: Closed-source generalist VLA powering Apptronik Apollo and other commercial humanoids.
- Methods: Gemini 2.0 backbone; Gemini Robotics-ER variant adds embodied reasoning + agentic tool use.
- Results: Smooth real-world manipulation; on-device variant deployable.
- Limitations: Closed weights; cloud dependency; multimodal reasoning latency vs reactive control trade-off.

meta.title: Gemini Robotics: Bringing AI into the Physical World
meta.key_contribution: Closed-source generalist VLA powering Apptronik Apollo and other commercial humanoids.
meta.methods: Gemini 2.0 backbone; Gemini Robotics-ER variant adds embodied reasoning + agentic tool use.
meta.results: Smooth real-world manipulation; on-device variant deployable.
meta.limitations: Closed weights; cloud dependency; multimodal reasoning latency vs reactive control trade-off.

notes.content:
**Pi-0.5: A Vision-Language-Action Model with Open-World Generalization** (arXiv 2025; arxiv:2504.16054)
- Contribution: First VLA shown to clean entirely unseen real-world kitchens and bedrooms across repeated trials.
- Methods: Builds on Pi-0 flow-matching action expert; adds hierarchical sub-goal reasoning at the VLM layer.
- Results: 60-80% task-completion success in novel residential environments.
- Limitations: Long-horizon failures remain dominant; teleop data still expensive; not yet a deployable household product.

meta.title: Pi-0.5: A Vision-Language-Action Model with Open-World Generalization
meta.key_contribution: First VLA shown to clean entirely unseen real-world kitchens and bedrooms across repeated trials.
meta.methods: Builds on Pi-0 flow-matching action expert; adds hierarchical sub-goal reasoning at the VLM layer.
meta.results: 60-80% task-completion success in novel residential environments.
meta.limitations: Long-horizon failures remain dominant; teleop data still expensive; not yet a deployable household product.

notes.content:
**Diffusion Policy: Visuomotor Policy Learning via Action Diffusion** (RSS 2023; arxiv:2303.04137)
- Contribution: +46.9% over prior SOTA across 12 tasks / 4 benchmarks.
- Methods: Receding-horizon prediction with DDPM-style conditional denoising on action sequences.
- Results: Robust to multimodal demonstrations; stable across diverse manipulation tasks.
- Limitations: Multi-step denoising slows inference; cross-embodiment transfer weak; needs depth for many tasks.
